questions.query: Across the agreements, list the **termination/cancellation** rules you can find.

runs.text:
**Termination/Cancellation Rules Across the Agreements**

---

### PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT

- **Term and Renewal**: The agreement is for an initial period of twelve months beginning on 2010-06-15 and is automatically renewed for successive one-year terms thereafter.
  - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)]

- **Termination by Either Party**: Either party may terminate the agreement upon sixty (60) days written notice.
  - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)]

- **Effect of Termination**:
  - Termination does not invalidate any existing rental agreements made by Agent on Owner's behalf.
  - Upon termination, Owner remains responsible for all obligations or expenses incurred by the Agent.
  - If the agreement is terminated or the property is sold, the Owner must honor all confirmed reservations.
  - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)]

---

### HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT

- **Duration and Termination**: The agreement remains in effect until either the owner or the pumper terminates the contract.
  - [HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT]

- **Change Notification**: In the event of a change in the contract, the owner must file a copy of any changes or a new service contract with the municipality and the County within ten (10) business days from the date of change.
  - [HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT]

---

### PURCHASE CONTRACT

- **Right to Cancel**: The customer may cancel the contract within 3 business days for a full refund. After this period, the deposit is forfeited.
  - [purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 10. Permitting > 11. Right to Cancel]

---

### BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY

- **Automatic Termination**:
  - The terms of the Limited Warranty terminate one (1) year or sixty (60) days after commencement, as applicable.
  - Any claims not made in writing within the applicable period (one year or sixty days) are not covered.
  - [BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.]

- **Transfer and Sale**:
  - The warranty is not transferable. If the first purchaser sells the home or moves out, the warranty automatically terminates.
  - [BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.]

- **Arbitration Clause**:
  - The provisions of the Arbitration Program survive any termination, amendment, or expiration of the warranty or related documents unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing.
  - [BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.]

---

**Complete List of Termination/Cancellation Rules:**

1. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT: Either party may terminate with 60 days written notice; does not affect existing rental agreements; Owner must fulfill obligations and honor confirmed reservations after termination.
2. HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT: Remains in effect until owner or pumper terminates; owner must notify municipality and County of changes within 10 business days.
3. PURCHASE CONTRACT: Customer may cancel within 3 business days for full refund; after 3 days, deposit is forfeited.
4. BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY: Terminates automatically after 1 year or 60 days as applicable; not transferable—terminates if first purchaser sells or moves out; claims must be made in writing within the coverage period; arbitration provisions survive termination unless otherwise agreed.

questions.query: Identify which documents reference **jurisdictions / governing law**.

runs.text:
**References to Jurisdictions / Governing Law by Document**

---

### PURCHASE CONTRACT

- **Section Referenced:** [purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 7. Applicable Law]
- **Governing Law Clause:** "This contract shall be governed by the laws of the State of Florida."
- **Additional Details:** The contract also specifies that neither party may assign the contract without the other's written consent.

---

### PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)

- **Section Referenced:** [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals) > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT:]
- **Governing Law Clause:** "This is the entire Agreement between the parties and shall be governed by the laws of the State of Hawaii."
- **Additional Details:** The agreement is binding upon the successors and assigns of Owner or Agent.

---

### BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY

- **Sections Referenced:** 
  - [BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.]
  - Multiple passages reference jurisdiction and governing law for arbitration and dispute resolution.
- **Governing Law and Jurisdiction Clauses:**
  - "Proceedings hereunder shall be governed by the provisions of the AAA or other rules agreed to by the parties. The arbitrator(s) shall otherwise resolve all Disputes in accordance with the applicable substantive law of the State of Idaho."
  - "Arbitrations shall be conducted in Pocatello, Idaho."
  - "No provision of, nor the exercise of any rights under this arbitration clause shall limit the right of any Party to: (1) pursue a claim within the venue and jurisdiction of the Small Claims Department of the Magistrate Division of the District Courts of the State of Idaho as defined at Idaho Code § 1-2301, for claims for money which do not exceed $3,000..."
  - "With exception of the remedies preserved herein (which includes claims for money within the jurisdiction limits of the small claims court of the State of Idaho) all disputes are subject to binding arbitration."
  - "Judgment upon any award rendered hereunder may be entered in any court having jurisdiction."

---

### HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT

- **Section Referenced:** [HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT]
- **Jurisdiction Reference:** The contract references compliance with "WI Code SPS 383.21(2)5" and filing with the "County of Washburn."
- **Note:** While this references a specific county and state code (Wisconsin), it does not contain a standard governing law clause but does reference jurisdictional compliance.

---

**Complete List of Documents Referencing Jurisdictions / Governing Law:**

1. **PURCHASE CONTRACT** (State of Florida)
2. **PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT** (State of Hawaii)
3. **BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY** (State of Idaho; Pocatello, Idaho; Idaho Code; AAA rules)
4. **HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT** (County of Washburn, Wisconsin; WI Code SPS 383.21(2)5)

All four documents contain references to jurisdictions or governing law.

questions.query: Summarize "who pays what" across the set (fees/charges/taxes).

runs.text:
Below is a summary of "who pays what" (fees, charges, taxes) across the set, organized by contractual relationship and role, with specific details and terminology preserved.

---

**1. Customer (Fabrikam Inc.) – Lift Purchase and Installation (Contoso Lifts LLC)**

- **Total Contract Price:** Customer pays $29,900.00 for the lift purchase and installation, as specified in [purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 2. Payment Terms] and [contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE].
    - **Installment Schedule:**
        - $20,000.00 upon signing the contract.
        - $7,000.00 upon delivery.
        - $2,900.00 upon completion.
- **Additional Costs:** Customer is responsible for any additional compliance costs related to the lift purchase contract.
- **Default Consequences:** Failure to pay may result in legal action; legal fees are recoverable by the Contractor ([purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 3. Changes > 4. Customer Default]).
- **Site Preparation:** Costs for site preparation (electrical hookup, concrete pad, hoistway) are to be provided by others, not the Contractor.
- **Refunds:** Customer may cancel within 3 business days for a full refund; after that, the deposit is forfeited.

---

**2. Owner – Property Management Agreement (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)**

- **Start-Up Fee:** Owner pays Agent a non-refundable start-up fee of $250.00 upon execution of the agreement ([PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT...]).
- **Management Fees/Commissions:**
    - 25% of gross revenues for management services for short-term/vacation rentals (reservations <180 days).
    - 10% of gross revenues for management services for long-term leases (leases >180 days).
- **Repair/Upgrade/Renovation Fees:**
    - 10% fee on the total charge for repairs, upgrades, and/or renovations.
    - $35/hour charge to schedule repairs, upgrades, renovations, replacement of appliances, carpets, curtains, drapes, and/or to escort contractors, repair persons, technicians, and handymen.
- **Advertising Charges:**
    - Pro-ration charge for advertising for short-term (vacation rentals): $75.00/month.
    - Agent pays advertising charges and passes the cost to Owner on monthly proceeds statements.
- **Administration and Accounting:** Minimum charge of $50.00/month (not including tax preparation/returns/forms).
- **Credit Card/Bankcard Fees:** Agent is allowed to deduct credit card and/or bankcard fees from Owner’s proceeds.
- **Taxes:** Agent will assess/charge the Owner the Hawaii Gross Excise tax (currently 4.712%) on all above fees, commissions, and charges.
- **Other Obligations:** Owner is responsible for all obligations or expenses incurred by Agent under the agreement, even after termination.
- **Insurance:** Owner must provide liability insurance coverage for the property (minimum $300,000 for bodily injury, $25,000 for property damage to others); Agent must be named as additional insured.
- **Tax Documentation:** Owner must provide Agent with General Excise Tax License and U.S. Federal Tax ID or Social Security number.

---

**3. Owner – Holding Tank Servicing Contract**

- **Servicing Charges:** Owner pays the pumper for charges incurred in servicing the holding tank(s), as mutually agreed.
- **Access Maintenance:** Owner agrees to maintain the all-weather access road or drive for pumper access.

---

**4. Arbitration and Legal Fees**

- **Arbitration Costs:** In the event of arbitration (e.g., under the Builders Limited Warranty), each party pays one-half of the cost and fees of arbitration and their respective attorney’s fees.

---

|           | $75/mo advertising; $50/mo admin; credit card/bankcard fees; Hawaii GET (4.712%);             |
|           | all Agent-incurred expenses; insurance; holding tank servicing charges; access maintenance     |
| Agent     | Pays advertising charges (passed to Owner); may deduct card fees from Owner’s proceeds         |
| Pumper    | Submits service reports to County; charges Owner for servicing                                 |

questions.query: What obligations are explicitly described as **reporting / record-keeping**?

runs.text:
Below is a complete list of obligations explicitly described as **reporting / record-keeping** in the available documents, organized by thematic area:

---

### 1. Owner-Agent Relationships in Property Rental Agreements

**A. Monthly Statements and Financial Records**
- The Agent is required to provide the Owner with a **monthly statement of income and expenses for the property**. [(PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals))]

**B. Filing of Contracts and Amendments**
- The Owner must **file a copy of the service contract with the County of Washburn**, as required under WI Code SPS 383.21(2)5. [(HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT)]
- In the event of a change in the contract, the Owner must **file a copy of any changes to the service contract or a copy of a new service contract with the municipality and the County** named above within ten (10) business days from the date of change. [(HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT)]

---

### 2. Property Management, Inspection, and Maintenance Documentation

**A. Servicing and Inspection Reports**
- The Pumper is obligated to **submit to the County, reports for the servicing of the holding tank(s) in the manner specified by the County**. [(HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT)]
- The Pumper’s report must include:
  - The name and address of the person responsible for servicing the holding tank.
  - The name of the owner of the holding tank.
  - The location of the property on which the holding tank is installed.
  - The sanitary permit number issued for the holding tank.
  - The dates on which the holding tank was serviced.
  - The volumes in gallons of the contents pumped from the holding tank for each servicing.
  - The disposal sites to which the contents from the holding tank were delivered.
  - The condition of the holding tank and components at the time of inspection.
  [(HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT)]

**B. Property Condition Reports**
- The Agent agrees to **complete an initial property inspection and prepare a condition report of the unit**. [(PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals))]

**C. Invoices and Proceeds Statements**
- The Agent must **provide the invoice(s) from the media, pay the charges, and pass on the cost to the Owner on the monthly proceeds statements**. [(PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals))]

---

### 3. Tax and Regulatory Information

- The Owner is required to **provide the Agent with the Owner's General Excise Tax License** and **U.S. Federal Tax ID or Social Security number**. [(PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals))]

---

**Summary Table of Explicit Reporting / Record-Keeping Obligations:**

| Obligation                                                                 | Responsible Party | Frequency/Timing                | Details/Contents                                                                                 |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Monthly statement of income and expenses                                   | Agent             | Monthly                          | Statement to Owner                                                                               |
| Filing contract with County (WI Code SPS 383.21(2)5)                       | Owner             | Upon contract execution          | Copy of contract to County of Washburn                                                           |
| Filing changes/new contract with municipality and County                   | Owner             | Within 10 business days of change| Copy of changes or new contract                                                                  |
| Servicing reports for holding tank(s)                                      | Pumper            | As specified by County           | Name/address of servicer, owner, property location, permit number, service dates, volumes, etc.  |
| Initial property inspection and condition report                           | Agent             | At start of management           | Condition report of unit                                                                         |
| Providing media invoices on monthly proceeds statements                    | Agent             | Monthly                          | Invoices for advertising expenses                                                                |
| Providing General Excise Tax License and Tax ID/Social Security number     | Owner             | Upon request/contract start      | Regulatory identification numbers to Agent                                                       |

---

This list includes every explicit reporting and record-keeping obligation found in the available documents.

questions.query: What remedies / dispute-resolution mechanisms are described?

runs.text:
The remedies and dispute-resolution mechanisms described in the available documents are as follows:

---

**1. Binding Arbitration (Primary Dispute Resolution Mechanism)**
- **Scope:** All disputes (as defined) that cannot be litigated in the Small Claims Court of the State of Idaho are subject to binding arbitration. This includes any action, dispute, claim, or controversy of any kind, whether founded in contract, tort, statutory or common law, equity, or otherwise, arising out of or in connection with the construction or purchase of the home, the warranty, or related agreements ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.], passages 1, 4, 5, 11).
- **Administration:** Arbitration is to be administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) or another mutually agreeable administrator, following the AAA Building Arbitration Rules or other mutually agreed rules (passages 5, 8, 13).
- **Location and Timing:** Arbitrations are to be conducted in Pocatello, Idaho, with a goal to conclude within 180 days of filing. Arbitrators may impose sanctions for delays (passages 14, 12).
- **Selection of Arbitrator:** Parties are to select a neutral, qualified arbitrator. If they cannot agree, AAA provisions apply (passage 15).
- **Consolidation:** Arbitrators may consolidate multiple party claims or related disputes, including those involving subcontractors or suppliers (passages 7, 15).
- **Costs:** Each party pays half the arbitration costs and their own attorney fees (passage 9).
- **Finality:** Once decided by arbitration, disputes cannot be brought to court except to enforce the arbitrator’s decision (passages 5, 8, 11).
- **Governing Law:** Arbitrators resolve disputes according to the substantive law of the State of Idaho. Judgments on awards may be entered in any court with jurisdiction (passages 10, 13).

---

**2. Preserved Remedies (Exceptions to Arbitration)**
- **Small Claims Court:** Parties retain the right to pursue claims for money within the jurisdictional limits of the Small Claims Department of the Magistrate Division of the District Courts of the State of Idaho (currently for claims not exceeding $3,000, per Idaho Code § 1-2301) ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.], passages 1, 6).
- **Claims of Lien and Foreclosure:** Parties may pursue claims of lien or foreclose a lien or mortgage against real or personal property collateral or security, as necessary to preserve such remedies or enforce arbitration awards, or to obtain a personal or deficiency award (passages 2, 6).
- **Self-Help Remedies:** Parties may exercise self-help remedies, including repossession and set-off rights (passages 2, 3, 6).
- **Provisional or Ancillary Remedies:** Parties may seek provisional or ancillary remedies such as injunctive relief, sequestration, attachment, claim and delivery, replevin, garnishment, or appointment of a receiver from a court having jurisdiction (passages 2, 3).

---

**3. Enforcement and Sanctions**
- **Compelling Arbitration:** Any party who fails to submit to binding arbitration after a lawful demand must bear all costs and reasonable attorneys’ fees incurred by the other party in obtaining a stay of judicial proceedings and compelling arbitration (passages 5, 11).
- **Sanctions for Delay:** Arbitrators are empowered to impose sanctions for failure to proceed within established timelines (passage 12).

---

**4. Additional Remedies and Legal Actions**
- **Legal Action for Non-Payment:** Failure to pay may result in legal action, and legal fees are recoverable by the contractor ([Thematic Context], [Financial Terms and Service Obligations in Property Contracts]).
- **Warranty Claims:** For defects covered by the limited warranty, the builder will repair or replace the item at no charge within sixty (60) days, or longer if delays are caused by weather, labor, or material shortages ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.], [Thematic Context]).

---

**Summary Table of Remedies / Dispute-Resolution Mechanisms**

| Mechanism / Remedy                       | Scope / Details                                                                                   |
|------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Binding Arbitration                      | All disputes not eligible for small claims court; AAA rules; final and binding; Idaho law applies |
| Small Claims Court                       | Claims for money ≤ $3,000 (Idaho Code § 1-2301)                                                   |
| Claims of Lien / Foreclosure             | To preserve or enforce security interests or arbitration awards                                    |
| Self-Help Remedies                       | Repossession, set-off rights                                                                      |
| Provisional / Ancillary Remedies         | Injunction, sequestration, attachment, claim & delivery, replevin, garnishment, receivership      |
| Legal Action for Non-Payment             | Contractor may recover legal fees                                                                 |
| Warranty Repair/Replacement              | Builder repairs/replaces covered defects within 60 days (or as delayed by circumstances)          |
| Sanctions for Delay in Arbitration       | Arbitrator may impose sanctions for failure to proceed                                            |
| Enforcement of Arbitration Award         | Judgment may be entered in any court with jurisdiction                                            |

This list includes all remedies and dispute-resolution mechanisms described in the available documents.

questions.query: Summarize all explicit **notice / delivery** mechanisms (written notice, certified mail, phone, filings) mentioned.

runs.text:
Below is a summary of all explicit **notice / delivery** mechanisms (written notice, certified mail, phone, filings) mentioned across the provided documents, organized by type and context:

---

### 1. Written Notice

- **Defect Notification (Warranty Claims):**
  - The Buyer/Owner must write a letter describing the defect to the Builder and send it by certified mail return receipt requested to the Builder’s office address. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])
  - Any and all claims must be made in writing within the applicable warranty period (one year or sixty days). Claims not made in writing are not covered. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])
  - Upon receipt of the buyer’s or owner’s written report of a defect, the Builder will repair or replace the item if covered. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])

- **Scheduling Service Calls:**
  - The Buyer/Owner must tell the Builder in writing what times during the day they will be at home, so the Builder can schedule service calls. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])

- **Property Listing Notification:**
  - The Owner must provide the Agent with written notification within five (5) business days of a listing agreement if the property is for sale. ([PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)])

- **Contract Changes:**
  - Any changes to the contract must be agreed to in writing. ([purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 3. Changes])

---

### 2. Certified Mail

- **Defect Notification (Warranty Claims):**
  - The written letter describing the defect must be sent by certified mail return receipt requested to the Builder’s office address. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])

---

### 3. Phone Notification

- **Emergency Reports:**
  - If an emergency exists or a delay will cause extra damage (e.g., a burst pipe), the Buyer/Owner must telephone the Builder immediately. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])
  - Only emergency reports will be taken by phone. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])
  - Failure to promptly notify the Builder of an emergency by phone relieves the Builder of all liability for replacement, repair, and other damages. ([BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY > BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY WITH ARBITRATION > 2. Term.])

---

### 4. Filings with Authorities

- **Service Contract Changes:**
  - In the event of a change in the service contract, the owner agrees to file a copy of any changes or a new service contract with the municipality and the County named above within ten (10) business days from the date of change. ([HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT])
  - The owner agrees to file a copy of the contract with the County of Washburn, as required under WI Code SPS 383.21(2)5. ([HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT > HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT])

---

### 5. Other Contact Methods (for Inquiries/Payments)

- **Invoice Inquiries:**
  - For invoice questions, contact is provided via phone and email, but this is for payment inquiries, not formal notice. ([contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE])

---

**Summary Table of Notice/Delivery Mechanisms:**

| Mechanism         | Context/Trigger                                   | Required Form/Details                                  |
|-------------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| Written Notice    | Defect claims, scheduling, property listing, changes | Letter, written report, written notification, written agreement |
| Certified Mail    | Defect claims (warranty)                          | Certified mail, return receipt requested               |
| Phone             | Emergency warranty issues                         | Immediate telephone call; only emergencies by phone     |
| Filing            | Service contract changes                          | File copy with municipality/County within 10 business days |
| Email/Phone (info)| Invoice/payment inquiries (not formal notice)      | Contact details provided for questions                 |

---

**Key Details:**
- Written notice is required for most non-emergency communications, including warranty claims, scheduling, and contract changes.
- Certified mail is specifically required for warranty defect notifications.
- Phone is only accepted for emergency warranty issues.
- Filings with government authorities are required for service contract changes.
- Email/phone contact is provided for invoice questions, but not for formal contractual notice.

Below is a complete summary of all explicit **insurance, indemnity, and hold harmless** clauses found in the available documents, organized by thematic context and preserving all specific details:

---

### 1. Owner’s Liability Insurance Requirements

- **PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals):**
  - The Owner is required to provide Liability Insurance coverage for the property with minimum limits of **$300,000 for bodily injury** and **$25,000 for property damage to others**.
  - The **Agent shall be named as additional insured** and shall be furnished with a copy of the insurance policy and a certificate of insurance.
    - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)] (f)
    - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals)] (f) (duplicate clause, confirming requirement)
    - [Contoso-Fabrikam Contractual and Property Relationships] (thematic context summary)

---

### 2. Indemnity and Hold Harmless Provisions

- **PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals):**
  - The Owner agrees that the **Agent shall not be liable for any claim for loss or injury to any person or property located on the property subject to this agreement**, except for loss or injury caused by Agent's **gross negligence or wilful misconduct**.
  - The **Owner shall hold Agent harmless and indemnify Agent for any such claim or liability**.
    - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals) > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT:]
    - [PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT (Short Term and/or Vacation/Holiday Rentals) > PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT:] (duplicate clause, confirming requirement)
    - [Legal Disputes and Contractual Relationships in Business Transactions] (thematic context summary)
    - [Financial Terms and Service Obligations in Property Contracts] (thematic context summary)

---

### 3. Risk of Loss (Related Clause)

- **PURCHASE CONTRACT:**
  - **Contractor holds risk until delivery. Customer holds risk thereafter.**
    - [purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 3. Changes > 5. Risk of Loss]
  - This clause allocates risk but does not explicitly use "insurance," "indemnity," or "hold harmless" language.

---

### 4. Additional Notes

- **No explicit insurance, indemnity, or hold harmless clauses** were found in the BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY or other sections outside the PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT and PURCHASE CONTRACT as detailed above.

---

runs.text:
**Summary of Each Document’s Main Purpose**

**PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT**  
The PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT sets out the terms and responsibilities under which an Agent manages, rents, and maintains an Owner’s property, including financial arrangements, property inspections, reporting, and the handling of reservations and administrative fees for short-term or vacation rentals.

**HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT**  
The HOLDING TANK SERVICING CONTRACT defines the obligations of the property owner and the pumper for the regular servicing, maintenance, and reporting of a property's holding tank, ensuring compliance with county and state regulations and specifying access, payment, and documentation requirements.

**BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY**  
The BUILDERS LIMITED WARRANTY provides the Buyer/Owner with a one-year warranty from the Builder covering defects in materials or workmanship for specified home components, outlining the terms, exclusions, and procedures for making warranty claims, as well as arbitration provisions for dispute resolution.

**purchase_contract**  
The purchase_contract is a legally binding agreement between Contoso Lifts LLC and Fabrikam Inc. detailing the sale, installation, and payment terms for a vertical platform lift, including scope of work, warranty, permitting, and cancellation provisions.

questions.query: What is the invoice’s **IBAN / SWIFT (BIC)** for international payments?

runs.text:
**Framing from Thematic Context**

The thematic context indicates that the invoice does not list an IBAN or SWIFT (BIC) code for international payments. This is a direct summary of the available information regarding international payment details.

**Findings from Document Evidence**

A review of the document evidence, including all sections of the invoice ([contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE]) and the purchase contract, shows no mention of an IBAN or SWIFT (BIC) code. The payment instructions provided are:
- "Make all checks payable to Contoso Lifts LLC"
- "Payments can also be made via our online portal: https://ww.contosolifts.com/portal/pay"

No section of the invoice or contract provides any bank account details, IBAN, or SWIFT (BIC) code for wire transfers or international payments.

**Summary**

The invoice does not provide an IBAN or SWIFT (BIC) code for international payments. Payment options listed are by check or via the online portal. If international payment details are required, they are not included in the available documents.

**Conclusion**

The requested information was not found in the available documents.

- The community summary explicitly states: "The invoice from Contoso Lifts LLC does not list a VAT or Tax ID number."

**Document Evidence**
- [contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE]: The invoice includes details such as INVOICE # 1256003, CUSTOMER ID #: 4905201, dates, payment instructions, and company contact information, but there is no mention of a VAT or Tax ID number.
- [contoso_lifts_invoice > [Letterhead]]: The company address and contact details are provided, but no VAT or Tax ID number is listed.
- [purchase_contract > section_0]: The contract between Contoso Lifts LLC and Fabrikam Inc. includes addresses and payment terms, but does not reference a VAT or Tax ID number for the vendor.

**Cross-Document Comparison**
- contoso_lifts_invoice: no VAT or Tax ID number found.
- purchase_contract: no VAT or Tax ID number found.
- PROPERTY MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT: references to providing tax IDs in general, but not for Contoso Lifts LLC or this invoice.

**Conclusion**
The requested information was not found in the available documents. The invoice from Contoso Lifts LLC does not list a VAT or Tax ID number.

questions.query: What is the invoice’s **bank account number** for ACH/wire payments?

runs.text:
**Bank Account Number for ACH/Wire Payments**

**Thematic Context: Payment Instructions**
The invoice emphasizes that "all checks [should be] payable to Contoso Lifts LLC" and provides an online payment portal (https://ww.contosolifts.com/portal/pay) for electronic payments. However, it is specifically noted that the invoice does not list a bank account number for ACH or wire payments.

**Document Evidence: Payment Details**
- [contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE] repeatedly instructs payment by check to Contoso Lifts LLC and offers the online portal for payments. There is no mention of a bank account number, routing number, or any ACH/wire transfer instructions in any of the invoice passages.
- Contact information for payment questions is provided (Jim Contoso, phone and email), but no banking details are included.
- The purchase contract section on payment terms ([purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 2. Payment Terms]) describes installment amounts and milestones but does not reference bank account information or ACH/wire payment instructions.

**Summary**
The invoice and related documents do not provide a bank account number for ACH or wire payments. Payment options are limited to checks payable to Contoso Lifts LLC and the online payment portal. For ACH/wire payment details, the documents direct the customer to contact the salesperson, but no actual bank account number is disclosed.

**Conclusion**
The requested information was not found in the available documents.

questions.query: What is the purchase contract’s required **wire transfer / ACH instructions**?

runs.text:
**Wire Transfer / ACH Instructions in the Purchase Contract**

**1. Explicit Payment Methods Required by the Contract and Invoice**

The purchase contract and associated invoice do **not** provide any explicit wire transfer or ACH instructions. Instead, the required payment methods are as follows:

- **Check Payment:** The contract and invoice specify that all checks should be made payable to Contoso Lifts LLC.  
  - [contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE]: "Make all checks payable to Contoso Lifts LLC"
  - [purchase_contract > section_0 > PURCHASE CONTRACT > 2. Payment Terms]: Payment milestones are listed, but no wire/ACH details are provided.

- **Online Portal Payment:** Payments can also be made via Contoso Lifts LLC's online portal at:  
  - https://ww.contosolifts.com/portal/pay  
  - [contoso_lifts_invoice > section_0 > INVOICE]: "Payments can also be made via our online portal: https://ww.contosolifts.com/portal/pay"

**2. Absence of Wire Transfer / ACH Instructions**

- There are **no wire transfer or ACH instructions** (such as bank name, routing number, or account number) included in the purchase contract or invoice.
- The thematic context confirms: "No explicit wire transfer or ACH instructions are provided in the purchase contract or invoice; only check and online portal payment methods are specified."

**3. Payment Terms and Contact Information**

- The contract price is $29,900.00, payable in three installments: $20,000.00 upon signing, $7,000.00 upon delivery, and $2,900.00 upon completion.
- For payment questions, the invoice provides a contact: Jim Contoso, (305) 490-5997, jim@contosolifts.com.

**Summary Table of Payment Methods Specified**

| Payment Method         | Details Provided? | Instructions/Details                                                                 |
|-----------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Check                 | Yes               | Make all checks payable to Contoso Lifts LLC                                         |
| Online Portal         | Yes               | https://ww.contosolifts.com/portal/pay                                               |
| Wire Transfer / ACH   | No                | No instructions, account numbers, or routing details provided in contract or invoice |

**Conclusion**

The purchase contract does **not** require or provide instructions for wire transfer or ACH payments. Only check payments and online portal payments are specified as acceptable methods. If wire transfer or ACH payment is desired, you must contact Contoso Lifts LLC directly for instructions, as none are included in the contract or invoice.
